Daily DAP Accessibility Report — 2026-04-08#

Run ID: run-2026-04-08-31ffb56ff4 · Status: success · Source data: 2026-04-06 · Generated: 2026-04-08T09:44:02.255Z

View HTML/CSS/JS Code Quality report for this date →

About These Reports#

The Digital Analytics Program (DAP) is a U.S. government analytics service that collects website traffic data across participating federal agencies. DAP tracks page views, visitor counts, and usage patterns for hundreds of government websites, providing transparency into how the public engages with federal digital services. The traffic data is publicly available at analytics.usa.gov.

This report measures the quality and accessibility of the top 100 most-visited U.S. government URLs as reported by DAP. Each day, Lighthouse scans are run against these URLs to measure:

  • Performance - How fast pages load for users (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Accessibility - How well pages work for users with disabilities, following WCAG guidelines (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Best Practices - Whether pages follow modern web development standards (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • SEO - How well pages are optimized for search engines (scores 0-100, higher is better)

Accessibility findings come from axe-core, the industry-standard accessibility testing engine embedded in Lighthouse. The axe findings surface specific WCAG violations such as missing alternative text, insufficient color contrast, and missing form labels that make government websites harder to use for people with disabilities.

Traffic data reflects daily visitor counts from DAP. URLs are ranked by page load count, ensuring the most-used government pages are prioritized for quality measurement.

Accessibility Trend Narrative#

Over the past 30 days of data (2026-03-09 to 2026-04-07), government website accessibility scores have remained stable (-0.46 points, from 91.98 to 91.52). Performance scores have improved (+6.14 points, from 48.02 to 54.16).

Today's aggregate accessibility score of 91.68 reflects the mean Lighthouse accessibility score across 100 successfully scanned government URLs. A score above 90 indicates generally strong compliance with WCAG automated checks, though manual testing is always recommended to fully assess accessibility.

Human impact narratives for top issues

The following narratives describe the real-world impact of each accessibility barrier on people with disabilities. These barriers are not abstract technical failures; they prevent citizens from independently accessing government services.

target-size: Digital Motor Access

Small touch targets act as a digital gatekeeper, excluding individuals with tremors, arthritis, or limited dexterity from accessing essential services independently. These technical failures transform a routine interaction into a source of failure, stripping away the autonomy of citizens who require a frictionless, accessible interface to participate in digital life. The approximately 58 million Americans with ambulatory or self-care disabilities are disproportionately impacted by inadequate touch target sizing on government mobile websites.

Affected groups:

  • People with Parkinson's disease, arthritis, or hand tremors
  • Older adults with reduced fine motor control
  • People with motor disabilities using alternative pointing devices
  • People in situational impairment contexts (e.g., commuting, holding a child)

heading-order: Document Structure Navigation

Screen reader users navigate complex government websites primarily through heading structure, using headings as a table of contents to jump between sections. Skipped heading levels break the logical document outline, causing confusion about the hierarchy of information and forcing users to re-read sections to understand the relationship between topics, adding significant time and effort to information-gathering tasks.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

landmark-one-main: Primary Content Identification

Without a main landmark, screen reader users lose their most efficient way to skip to the primary content of a government page. Every page visit requires tabbing through the entire navigation structure to reach the actual content, a significant time and effort burden that accumulates across repeated visits to government websites for citizens managing ongoing benefit cases or legal matters.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

color-contrast: Visual Information Access

Low contrast text is one of the most pervasive barriers on government websites, rendering critical information invisible to the approximately 26 million Americans with low vision or color blindness. When agency announcements, form instructions, error messages, or legal notices are displayed in insufficient contrast, affected citizens are denied equal access to the information they need to exercise their rights and access public services.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision including age-related vision loss
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)

label-content-name-mismatch: Voice Control Activation

When the accessible name of an element does not match its visible text, voice control users cannot activate it using the words they see on screen. Saying "click Submit" to a button whose visual label is "Submit" but whose accessible name is "submitbtn" results in no action. This mismatch silently breaks voice control for citizens with motor disabilities who depend on this input method to interact with government websites.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People with low vision relying on screen magnifiers
  • People who are deaf using keyboard navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using voice control software

link-name: Link Purpose Clarity

Links without accessible names are completely useless to screen reader users who navigate government pages by jumping between links. An unnamed link could lead anywhere, and activating it unknowingly could trigger downloads, open unexpected pages, or initiate unintended processes. On government websites, unnamed links undermine the informed consent principle by preventing citizens from knowing where a link will take them.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

image-alt: Visual Content Access

Images without alternative text are completely invisible to blind users and to search engines. On government websites, informational images, diagrams, infographics, and document screenshots without alt text deprive blind citizens of the same information available to sighted visitors. Critical details like emergency instructions, benefit eligibility charts, or form procedure diagrams can be entirely inaccessible, putting these citizens at a disadvantage in accessing government services and information.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools

listitem: List Item Semantic Context

Orphaned list items outside of list containers lose their semantic meaning and list navigation properties. Screen reader users rely on list structure to understand that a set of items is related and to count the total number of items. Government navigation menus, step lists, and option groups presented as invalid lists are harder to parse and navigate efficiently.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities navigating by keyboard

button-name: Button Discoverability

Unlabeled buttons are silent black holes in the government digital experience. When a screen reader announces a button with no name, blind users cannot know what action it will perform. Submit, cancel, search, and navigation buttons without accessible names prevent citizens from completing applications, accessing benefits, or finding essential government resources independently.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools

link-in-text-block: Color-Independent Link Identification

When links are distinguished only by color, people with color vision deficiency cannot identify them as links and may miss critical references to additional information, forms, or resources. On government websites, overlooked links in informational content can mean missing essential instructions, deadlines, or procedures that affect a citizen's ability to access services or comply with requirements.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision who may not perceive color differences
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)
Disability icon key
Without Vision — People who are blind or have no functional vision (1.0% of U.S. population — ~3,400,000 Americans)
Limited Vision — People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast (2.4% of U.S. population — ~8,100,000 Americans)
Without Perception of Color — People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness) (4.3% of U.S. population — ~14,500,000 Americans)
Without Hearing — People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content (0.3% of U.S. population — ~1,100,000 Americans)
Limited Hearing — People with hearing loss who may struggle with audio without accommodations (3.5% of U.S. population — ~11,900,000 Americans)
Without Speech — People who cannot use speech or voice-based input effectively (0.5% of U.S. population — ~1,700,000 Americans)
Limited Manipulation — People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity (2.2% of U.S. population — ~7,600,000 Americans)
Limited Reach and Strength — People with limited reach, strength, or stamina (5.8% of U.S. population — ~19,600,000 Americans)
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities — People with cognitive, learning, or language differences (4.7% of U.S. population — ~15,900,000 Americans)

These icons show which groups of people with disabilities are excluded by each accessibility barrier. Where page view data is available, each icon shows an estimated number of people potentially excluded (page loads for affected URLs × disability prevalence rate from U.S. Census ACS 2023). Hover over or focus an icon to see the full estimate and methodology. Icons follow the Section 508 Functional Performance Criteria and the equivalent EU EN 301 549 v3.2.1 Table B.2 categories.

Prevalence data: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; supplemental data from CDC, NIDCD, AFB, and NIH/NEI. U.S. population base: ~336,500,000 (2023 estimate). Reviewed annually.

See the Section 508 WCAG to FPC mapping for additional detail on how accessibility requirements map to functional needs.

Usability Heuristics Summary#

The most common accessibility violations map to Nielsen Norman Group's 10 usability heuristics. This table shows which heuristics are most affected by today's top axe-core violations, helping to identify where systemic usability barriers exist.

NN/g usability heuristics affected by today's top accessibility violations

6 Recognition rather than recall 5 94 heading-order, landmark-one-main, color-contrast, link-name, listitem

8 Aesthetic and minimalist design 5 72 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, image-alt, listitem, link-in-text-block

1 Visibility of system status 4 62 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, listitem, link-in-text-block

Americans Excluded by Disability Today#

Based on 68,880,131 page loads across successfully scanned government URLs and U.S. disability prevalence rates from the U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101 (2023), an estimated 4,990,000 Americans encountered an accessibility barrier on a government website today.

Each row shows the number of people in a disability group who visited a page with at least one accessibility issue that affects their group. Prevalence rates and U.S. population estimates are derived from the American Community Survey (ACS) and supplemental sources. These figures are rough estimates intended to illustrate the scale of accessibility barriers.

Estimated Americans excluded today by disability category (2023 Census data)
Icon Disability Group (Section 508 FPC) U.S. Prevalence U.S. Population Affected Page Loads with Barriers Est. Americans Excluded Today

Limited Manipulation 2.2% ~7,600,000 46,500,000 1,020,000
Without Perception of Color 4.3% ~14,500,000 21,700,000 930,000
Limited Vision 2.4% ~8,100,000 27,700,000 660,000
Without Vision 1.0% ~3,400,000 46,500,000 460,000
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities 4.7% ~15,900,000 4,940,000 230,000
Without Hearing 0.3% ~1,100,000 46,500,000 130,000

Sources: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; disability prevalence from ACS 2023 and supplemental CDC/NIDCD/NIH data. Reviewed annually.

Performance Impact on Americans#

Google defines a good Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) as under 2.5 seconds and recommends pages under 1.6 MB. The figures below estimate how much extra time Americans spend waiting, and how much extra data is transferred, because government websites fall short of these benchmarks. Calculations are based on 96 successfully scanned URLs with Lighthouse timing data.

Estimated performance impact vs. Google benchmarks for government URLs scanned on April 8, 2026
Metric Estimated total (April 8, 2026) Notes
Extra time waiting (vs 2.5s LCP benchmark) 12 years, 23 daysExtra time is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual LCP − 2.5s) × page loads. Total: 380,725,077 seconds. 380,725,077 seconds
Extra data transferred (vs 1.6 MB page weight benchmark) ~147.1 TB (6,116 copies of Wikipedia)Extra data is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual page weight − 1.6 MB) × page loads. Across 96 URLs with weight data

LCP and page weight are measured by Lighthouse. Wikipedia copy count uses a size of 24.05 GB per Wikipedia:Size of Wikipedia. These are rough estimates based on a sample of the top government URLs by traffic. Hover or focus the estimated totals for calculation details.

Content Density (Words per Megabyte)#

Words-per-Megabyte (WpM) measures how efficiently a page delivers readable content relative to its total download size. A low ratio indicates Digital Bloat—the page transfers far more data than the text it contains. Pages below 200 WpM are flagged for optimization review.

  • URLs with content metrics: 73
  • Average word count: 218
  • Average Words/MB: 91
  • URLs below 200 WpM threshold: 66
Content density for top URLs scanned on April 8, 2026 (up to 10 shown)

https://studentaid.gov 13 3

Word counts are extracted from the page’s main article content using Mozilla Readability, which strips navigation, sidebars, and ads. Page weight is the total resource size measured by Lighthouse. Pages below 200 WpM may impose unnecessary data costs on users on metered connections.

Detected Technologies#

Technology signals detected from the network requests loaded by each scanned URL. CMS detection identifies WordPress, Drupal, and Joomla from characteristic asset paths. USWDS detection identifies use of the U.S. Web Design System.

CMSURLsShare
Drupal27Sites: acquisition.gov, archives.gov, cbp.gov, cisa.gov, cms.gov, dea.gov, dhs.gov, epa.gov, faa.gov, fdic.gov, fs.usda.gov, fsa.usda.gov, gsa.gov, home.treasury.gov, justice.gov, medicare.gov, nhlbi.nih.gov, noaa.gov, ofac.treasury.gov, osha.gov, sam.gov, studentaid.gov, tsa.gov, usa.gov, uscis.gov, usgs.gov, uspto.gov27%

USWDS detected on 37 of 100 scanned URLs (37%).

Observed USWDS versions: 2.9.0Sites: fbi.gov, 3.8.0Sites: fdic.gov. The latest release is available at github.com/uswds/uswds/releases.

Third-Party JavaScript Services#

Third-party scripts detected from network requests loaded by scanned pages. These services may affect page performance, user privacy, and security posture.

12 services marked ⚠ tracking send user data to third-party servers and may have privacy implications for site visitors.

Accessibility Statements (M-24-08)#

OMB Memorandum M-24-08 requires each federal agency to publish a digital accessibility statement that includes contact information for accessibility issues, known limitations, and a link to the agency Section 508 program page.

62 of 100 domains (62%) checked have a detectable accessibility statement at a standard URL path.

Required Federal Links (M-17-06)#

OMB Memorandum M-17-06 and the 21st Century IDEA Act require federal agency public websites to provide links to a Privacy Policy, Contact page, and FOIA page. These were among the compliance criteria benchmarked by the performance.gov federal website performance initiative (no longer active, but the underlying requirements remain in force).

60 of 100 domains (60%) have all three required link types detectable at standard URL paths.

Section 508: Legal Requirements vs. Best Practices#

Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act was enacted in 1998 and requires U.S. federal agencies to make their electronic and information technology accessible to people with disabilities. The technical standards were significantly updated through the ICT Accessibility Standards refresh, which became effective in January 2018 — the same year Apple celebrated the 10th anniversary of the iPhone. That refresh incorporated WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published in 2008) as the baseline for web content.

The web has changed dramatically since then. What agencies are legally required to meet today is built on a standard from 2008 — the same era as the original iPhone. Agencies should treat WCAG 2.0 AA as the floor, not the ceiling.

Legal Requirement

WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published 2008)

Referenced by the Section 508 ICT Standards refresh (effective 2018). Agencies must meet these 38 success criteria to satisfy their Section 508 obligations for web content.

  • Text alternatives for non-text content
  • Captions and audio descriptions for multimedia
  • Keyboard accessibility and focus management
  • Minimum color contrast (4.5:1 for normal text)
  • No seizure-inducing content
  • Consistent navigation and labeling
  • Error identification and correction

Section 508 web content requirements (E205)

Best Practices

WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 Level AA (published 2018 and 2023)

These later versions add criteria that reflect how the web actually works today — mobile devices, cognitive accessibility, and modern authentication patterns — and are strongly recommended for any agency aiming to serve all Americans. The US Federal Government is one of the last to use the legacy WCAG 2.0 AA Standard, as it was developed ~2 decades ago.

  • WCAG 2.1 (2018): 17 new criteria for mobile, low vision, and cognitive accessibility
  • WCAG 2.2 (2023): 9 more criteria for authentication and cognitive tasks, removing the outdated "Parsing" criterion
  • W3C now recommends WCAG 2.2 as the current normative standard for new and updated content

What is new in WCAG 2.2 · WCAG 2.2 specification

The scores and findings in this report are based on automated checks aligned with WCAG 2.x rules surfaced by axe-core. Passing automated checks is necessary but not sufficient: manual testing with assistive technologies is required to verify true conformance and to catch barriers that automation cannot detect.

Take Action#

Today's scan identified 232 accessibility barriers across the most-visited U.S. government websites, affecting an estimated 4,990,000 Americans with disabilities. Here is how you can help.

  • Read the federal accessibility report. The 2025 Governmentwide Section 508 Assessment from the GSA Administrator details the state of federal accessibility compliance and the steps agencies are taking to improve.
  • Submit URLs you care about. Want specific government pages included in future scans? Submit them to the Open Scans project to help broaden coverage.
  • Test with free tools. Do your own automated and manual accessibility testing with Accessibility Insights and Google Lighthouse. Automated tools catch a significant portion of WCAG failures quickly; manual review with assistive technologies catches the rest.
  • Adopt and update the U.S. Web Design System (USWDS). The USWDS is already a strong foundation for accessible, consistent federal websites, but many agencies have not adopted it or are running outdated versions. Encourage your agency to adopt or upgrade so every American benefits from its accessibility work.
  • Hire people with disabilities. People with disabilities are the ultimate experts on digital barriers. Bringing them into design, engineering, and testing teams — not just as consultants but as full-time employees — leads to more accessible products and services for everyone.

Accessibility Findings

https://tools.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 68

Axe findings: 10

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name) DAP-c26fc51b

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~114KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~114,932 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 11,493,235 affected page loads) ~34KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~34,480 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 11,493,235 affected page loads) ~252KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~252,851 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 11,493,235 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-7ca376ec

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a#headingOneAnchor > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2 DAP-958bc2d3

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 3 DAP-958bc2d3

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-82af62ae

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Estimated ~114,932 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 11,493,235 affected page loads) ~34KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~34,480 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 11,493,235 affected page loads) ~252KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Element 1 DAP-4f198039

Element path: div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > nav > ul.nav-list

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) DAP-b319271f

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Disabilities affected: ~114KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Element 1 DAP-b8ca2593

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.qt-nav > a.nav-first-element

Snippet:

<a aria-expanded="false" role="menuitem" tabindex="0" aria-haspopup="true" class="nav-first-element menuitem" href="#">

Label: Quick Tools

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parents role not present: menu, menubar, group

  • Required ARIA parents role not present: menu, menubar, group

Element 3 DAP-68b31546

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.menuheader > a.menuitem

<a aria-expanded="false" class="menuitem" role="menuitem" tabindex="0" aria-haspopup="true" href="https://www.usps.com/manage/">

  • Required ARIA parents role not present: menu, menubar, group

Element 4 DAP-68b31546

Element path: nav > ul.nav-list > li.menuheader > a.menuitem

Snippet:

<a aria-expanded="false" class="menuitem" role="menuitem" tabindex="0" aria-haspopup="true" href="https://store.usps.com/store">

`[aria-*]` attributes do not have valid values (rule: aria-valid-attr-value) DAP-0819be8b

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-1abfb3a4

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-8c4fed15

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-dd951b79

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Accessibility Findings

https://nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 89

Axe findings: 4

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-08b676ee

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~57KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~57,179 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 5,717,896 affected page loads) ~17KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~17,154 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 5,717,896 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-26dff29d

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-dfced523

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button.hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger

Snippet:

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-c6127178

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Element path: div.slick-track > div.hds-meet-the-card > div.hds-meet-the-inner > a.hds-meet-the-image

Snippet:

<a href="https://plus.nasa.gov/scheduled-events/" target="_blank" class="hds-meet-the-image padding-right-3" rel="noopener" tabindex="0">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2 DAP-0887162f

Element path: div.grid-row > div.grid-col-12 > div.hds-image-download-wrapper > a.hds-image-download-link

Snippet:

<a class="hds-image-download-link" href="https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/art002e009288orig-1.jpg">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch) DAP-f1f3d3a0

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-84eeaaec

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-ebb2bc0f

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-5d46b830

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-3 > p > a.text-white

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.nlm.nih.gov/accessibility.html" class="text-white">

Label: Accessibility

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (77.3px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.8px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-5d46b830

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-3 > p > a.text-white

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.nlm.nih.gov/careers/careers.html" class="text-white">

Label: Careers

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (49.8px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.8px instead of at least 24px.

https://usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 68

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-4bdf91e9

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-29ea0262

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-474d58d2

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-f1542b28

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-2be6feaa

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-7b9fc94e

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~54KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~54,828 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 2,284,494 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-efeb19cc

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-6c2a019c

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) DAP-219167c9

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-9edc0d03

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-c24efba6

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-aee85114

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-491464b3

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-7b4fd568

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Estimated ~40,274 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 1,678,093 affected page loads) ~72KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Element 1 DAP-7263ff7e

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-e62a32c4

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Estimated ~78,870 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 1,678,093 affected page loads)

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-d36929f4

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

https://studentaid.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-b627e7e4

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~9KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~9,430 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,829 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads) ~20KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~20,746 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-1f24d785

Element path: div.home-banner > div.row > div.col-md-5 > h4#fsa_Title_PopularTasks

Snippet:

<h4 _ngcontent-vfb-c154="" id="fsa_Title_PopularTasks" class="text-left fsa-mb-px-16 fsa-color-white">

Label: POPULAR TOPICS

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-817c69a4

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~22KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~22,632 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads) ~40KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~40,549 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-25e0050e

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="/loan-simulator">

Label: explore and apply for other repayment plans

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2 DAP-25e0050e

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="/announcements-events/idr-court-actions">

Label: visit StudentAid.gov/courtactions

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 3 DAP-25e0050e

Element path: div.fsa-banner-content-body > span > div > a

Snippet:

<a href="/fsa-id/sign-in/landing/?redirectTo=/my-aid">

Label: Log in

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-3b00bfb3

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~20KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~20,746 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads) ~54KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~54,694 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 942,996 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-9bdbf19c

Element path: footer#fsa-universal-footer > section.fsa-top-links > a#fsa_Link_FooterLevel1SiteSurvey

Snippet:

<a class="fsa-footer-link fsa-delimited-link-mobile fsa-top-footer-links bold" id="fsa_Link_FooterLevel1SiteSurvey" href="javascript:window.KAMPYLE_ONSITE_SDK.showForm('308');">

Label: Site Survey

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (61px by 23px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 0px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-3e7fec37

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~7,626 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 762,559 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,288 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 762,559 affected page loads) ~16KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~16,776 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 762,559 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-2428d6f9

Element path: nav.mobile-navbar > div.mobile-navbar_top > div.d-flex > a#cdc-mobile-search-toggle

Snippet:

<a id="cdc-mobile-search-toggle" class="nav-link" href="#" aria-controls="cdc-mobile-search-form">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Skip links are not focusable. (rule: skip-link) DAP-b3698d5b

Including a skip link can help users skip to the main content to save time. Learn more about skip links.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~7,626 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 762,559 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-d7fc04fe

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-6eb16f7d

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-86bc430e

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~17KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-91b3be38

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-0d01a89e

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~7,320 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,196 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads) ~34KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~34,404 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b7c14034

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-f0437918

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~7,320 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,196 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-82cd40dd

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#us

Snippet:

<img id="us" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/US.png" usemap="#US_large_imagemap_800_500">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-f1fbd8ef

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#alaska

Snippet:

<img id="alaska" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/ak.png" usemap="#alaska_imagemap_130_115">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 3 DAP-f9471b99

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#hawaii

Snippet:

<img id="hawaii" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/hi.png" usemap="#hawaii_imagemap">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-8694972a

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~16KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~16,104 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads) ~42KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~42,456 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 732,007 affected page loads)

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-d40c6891

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Accessibility Findings

https://travel.state.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-99efae35

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~17KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~17,194 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 716,436 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-06ade13b

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-0296b6d2

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~6,605 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 660,490 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,981 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 660,490 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b6b8a0b7

Element path: div#tophat3Widget > nav#tophat3WidgetNavBar > div.container-fluid > button#tophat3HamburgerButton

Snippet:

<button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" id="tophat3HamburgerButton">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-379aa721

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~15KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~15,852 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 660,490 affected page loads) ~28KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~28,401 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 660,490 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-8210b588

Element path: section#callout-login > form#login > p.button-with-notes > input.eui-btn--round

Snippet:

<input type="submit" name="commit" value="Log in" class="eui-btn--round eui-btn--green" data-disable-with="Log in">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.07 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #20ce6f, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-0fce787d

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-d7a3ae4a

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~15KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~15,852 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 660,490 affected page loads) ~28KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~28,401 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 660,490 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-66ab7ee6

Element path: body.static_pages > div.container > div.home-info-box > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://earthdata.nasa.gov/about/daacs">

Label: DAACs

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.59:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #2276ac, surrounding text: #323232)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-056620d5

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~6,279 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 627,884 affected page loads) ~15KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~15,069 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 627,884 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,884 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 627,884 affected page loads) ~13KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~13,813 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 627,884 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-16a409b4

Element path: div.view-content > div.slick-list > div.slick-track > div#slick-slide00

Snippet:

<div class="slick-slide" data-slick-index="0" aria-hidden="true" role="tabpanel" id="slick-slide00" tabindex="-1" style="width: 412px;" aria-describedby="slick-slide-control00">

Label: Making America Safe Again USCIS’ end-of-year review demonstrates the impact of…

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-4ac63560

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-80cca99a

Affected elements (16):

Element 1 DAP-e1d41724

Element path: div.footer1 > ul.secondarynav > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://medlineplus.gov/about/">

Label: About MedlinePlus

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (110.9px by 21.4px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 21.4px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://plus.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 83

Axe findings: 5

Elements use prohibited ARIA attributes (rule: aria-prohibited-attr) DAP-7b77bac8

Using ARIA attributes in roles where they are prohibited can mean that important information is not communicated to users of assistive technologies. Learn more about prohibited ARIA roles.

Affected elements (6):

Element 1 DAP-9b9fef61

Element path: div#splide06 > div.grid-container > div.grid-row > div.banner--navigation-item

Snippet:

<div class="banner--navigation-item padding-0 margin-x-05 margin-y-1 tablet:margin-x-1…" tabindex="0" aria-label="Go to slide 2" aria-controls="">

Label: Go to slide 2

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • aria-label attribute cannot be used on a div with no valid role attribute.

Element 2 DAP-9b9fef61

Element path: div#splide06 > div.grid-container > div.grid-row > div.banner--navigation-item

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-cbadb8e4

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~5,577 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 557,692 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,673 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 557,692 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-b17f931b

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-234b943f

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button.hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger

Snippet:

<button class="hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger search-mobile color-carbon-20 menu-toggle" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="right">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-de89b71c

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Element 1 DAP-de70e417

Snippet:

<h3 class="heading-22 line-height-md">

Label: National Aeronautics and Space Administration

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-5ce675b4

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~13KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~13,385 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 557,692 affected page loads) ~23KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~23,981 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 557,692 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-15601c3d

Element path: header.grid-row > div.grid-row > span.font-size--x-small > a

Snippet:

<a href="/schedule">

Label: America: New York

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.41:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #f6f6f6, surrounding text: #d1d1d1)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-98d5f9d0

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

  • Element has no title attribute

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-b71125e4

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Affected elements (15):

Element 1 DAP-6b8ed34e

Element path: div.row > div.col-xs-12 > sup > a#password-tip

Snippet:

<a role="button" id="password-tip" tabindex="0" data-placement="top" data-remotecontent="/entreg/assets/html/tooltips/tool-tip-forgot-useralias.html" data-toggle="popover" aria-label="Retrieve your username" data-bs-original-title="Retrieve your username">

Label: Retrieve your username

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 21px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://mobile.arc.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 92

Axe findings: 2

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-5af8edb4

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4,106 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 410,620 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,232 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 410,620 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-e1c18416

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-c94f3d4e

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button.hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger

Snippet:

<button class="hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger search-mobile color-carbon-20 menu-toggle" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="right">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-e9d91808

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-4d2e97e5

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-c0c32991

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~7,706 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 321,067 affected page loads) ~13KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~13,806 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 321,067 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-dbbb92aa

Element path: div.usa-grid > div.usa-footer-logo > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.tsp.gov/contact/">

Label: contact us

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.5:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #d6d7d9, surrounding text: #aeb0b5)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Accessibility Findings

https://radar.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 77

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-63fbd253

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,823 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 282,342 affected page loads) ~847People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~847 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 282,342 affected page loads) ~13KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~13,270 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 282,342 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-be88516d

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-94dc23bb

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~6,776 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 282,342 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-36f6c272

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=0">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Accessibility Findings

https://my.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 89

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-1c5b51c5

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,810 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 281,024 affected page loads) ~843People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~843 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 281,024 affected page loads) ~6KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~6,183 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 281,024 affected page loads)

Label: Menu

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-d2302c08

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~6,745 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 281,024 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-04858f4d

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,height=device-height,user-scalable=no,initial-scale=1.0…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://tsa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 99

Axe findings: 1

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-1d6dadbd

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,747 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 274,702 affected page loads) ~824People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~824 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 274,702 affected page loads) ~6KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~6,043 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 274,702 affected page loads)

Accessibility Findings

https://nhlbi.nih.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Axe findings: 1

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-59cedeac

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~5,713 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 259,667 affected page loads) ~15KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Affected elements (5):

Element 1 DAP-c3f3f37b

Element path: nav.block > ul.menu > li.menu-item > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://eeo.nih.gov/no-fear-act" rel="noreferrer">

Label: No FEAR Act Data

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (116.4px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20.2px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-539ebd42

Element path: nav.block > ul.menu > li.menu-item > a.ext-icon

Snippet:

<a class="ext-icon" href="#">

Label: (link is external)

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (24px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 14px instead of at least 24px.

Element path: nav.block > ul.menu > li.menu-item > a.ext

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.hhs.gov/" rel="nofollow noopener noreferrer" class="ext" target="_blank">

Label: Health and Human Services

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (167.9px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20px instead of at least 24px.

Element 4 DAP-c3f3f37b

Element path: nav.block > ul.menu > li.menu-item > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.nih.gov/" rel="noreferrer">

Label: National Institutes of Health

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (181.1px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 18px instead of at least 24px.

Element 5 DAP-539ebd42

Element path: nav.block > ul.menu > li.menu-item > a.ext-icon

Snippet:

<a class="ext-icon" href="#">

Accessibility Findings

https://esta.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 85

Axe findings: 4

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-72867807

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,546 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads) ~764People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~764 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-27009710

Element path: div.toggle-language > span.dropdown > button#languageDrop > img

Snippet:

<img _ngcontent-ng-c4084404552="" src="assets/images/flags/usUkFlag.png">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-4d2fde10

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~6,109 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads) ~10KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~10,946 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-a5b23db0

Element path: div.col-xs-12 > ul > li#welcome.whoYouAre.1 > a.strong

Snippet:

<a tabindex="0" data-target="#visaModal" data-toggle="modal" class="strong esta-link" href="#">

Label: Visa Waiver Program

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.18:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00416a, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list) DAP-1eb5adbc

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,546 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads) ~764People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~764 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-d71436a1

Element path: div.container > div.row > div.col-xs-12 > ul

Snippet:

<ul _ngcontent-ng-c1211797416="">

Label: About ESTA and The Visa Waiver Program What is the difference between an ESTA …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: div
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-c3aa4ba1

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,546 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads) ~764People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~5,600 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 254,561 affected page loads)

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-756f8154

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,456 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 245,616 affected page loads) ~737People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~737 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 245,616 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-d406bcd7

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-417501325

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-417501325" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-417501325">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
