Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-dbfac03d

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,275 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 227,539 affected page loads) ~683People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~683 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 227,539 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-4b0d54f2

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-83a4c980

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button.hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger

Snippet:

<button class="hds-search-panel-mobile-trigger search-mobile color-carbon-20 menu-toggle" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="right">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-1e090eee

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Accessibility Findings

https://store.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 70

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-cc50ea1e

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,195 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 219,484 affected page loads) ~658People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element 1 DAP-48a6fa38

Snippet:

<ul class="nav-list" role="menubar">

Label: Skip Quick Tools Links Quick Tools Track a Package Informed Delivery …

  • Element has children which are not allowed: li[tabindex]
`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) DAP-81d032ab

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-f5383b9b

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Estimated ~5,268 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 219,484 affected page loads) ~9KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-675161a0

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Element 1 DAP-d114f32e

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

Accessibility Findings

https://nsopw.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 99

Axe findings: 1

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-1572b804

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

https://spc.noaa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 53

Axe findings: 8

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-9e537039

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,974 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads) ~7KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~7,120 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-b78616b0

<input type="text" id="query-field" name="inputstring" size="12" value="Local Forecast by ZIP/City, State" onfocus="this.value='';" onblur="this.value='Local Forecast by ZIP/City, State';">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

Element 1 DAP-d0cdbcfd

<iframe src="//www.facebook.com/plugins/likebox.php?href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com…" scrolling="yes" frameborder="1" style="border:none; overflow:hidden; width:500px; height:395px;" allowtransparency="true">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-34fc29ea

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~7,782 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads)

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-84ac16d9

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-3f1e53d3

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-485fce88

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,656 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads) ~497People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~497 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,643 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-ef8f516e

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="//www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header) DAP-42964e6b

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,656 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads) ~497People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~497 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 165,575 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-17306674

Element path: tbody > tr > td > table

Snippet:

<table cellspacing="1" cellpadding="2" border="0" width="100%">

Label: Hazard Wed (04/08) Thu (04/09) Fri (04/10) Sat (04/11) Sun (04/12) Mon (04/13) …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Some non-empty data cells do not have table headers

Accessibility Findings

https://opm.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-0190d646

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,568 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 156,834 affected page loads) ~471People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element 1 DAP-dda8e498

Element path: div.grid-container > div.grid-row > div.featured-audience > a

Snippet:

<a tabindex="-1" href="https://www.opm.gov/job-seekers/">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

  • Element has no title attribute

  • Element has no title attribute
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-46a39875

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,568 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 156,834 affected page loads) ~471People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~3,450 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 156,834 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-cea3da96

Element path: body#htmBody > a.usa-skipnav

Snippet:

<a class="usa-skipnav skipnav" href="#main-main-content" tabindex="1">

Label: Skip to main content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Accessibility Findings

https://ttp.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-cd9f6be4

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,557 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 155,665 affected page loads) ~467People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~3,425 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 155,665 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-a0cbd01b

Element path: div.nav-row > div.container > nav.navbar > a.navbar-toggle

Snippet:

<a type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#app-navbar" aria-expanded="false" class="navbar-toggle navbar-toggler collapsed">

Label: MENU

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-5fef0a0d

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Accessibility Findings

https://earthquake.usgs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 94

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-a0c91230

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Estimated ~7,207 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 153,349 affected page loads)

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute

Accessibility Findings

https://i94.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 5

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-36bf93e1

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,510 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 150,984 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,624 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 150,984 affected page loads) ~453People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~453 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 150,984 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,322 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 150,984 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-4fdf2ba4

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Element 2 DAP-4fdf2ba4

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-307136cb

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-0256ac01

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-3fb09f9a

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-fa45fcda

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Tables do not use `<caption>` instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute to indicate a caption. (rule: table-fake-caption) DAP-97a2bee5

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that tables use the actual caption element instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about captions.

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-415613a4

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Estimated ~5,993 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 127,521 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-01a58656

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-ef8eddce

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,805 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 127,521 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Affected elements (5):

Element 1 DAP-bb3d58ad

Element path: body#bdyMain > div#main > div#actionbar > a

Snippet:

<a href="?note">

Label: Notes

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (90px by 12.8px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 7.6px instead of at least 24px.

Snippet:

<a href="?faq">

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 7.6px instead of at least 24px.

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

ARIA input fields do not have accessible names (rule: aria-input-field-name) DAP-f8694bf1

When an input field doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn more about input field labels.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-c2170da9

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-64fe4ab5

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Accessibility Findings

https://tsaenrollmentbyidemia.tsa.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 85

Axe findings: 4

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-6c7c834d

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,177 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 117,722 affected page loads) ~353People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~353 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 117,722 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-d9bd14ac

Element path: div.col-12 > div.official-banner > div#accordion > img.ues-usa-img

Snippet:

<img src="/images/us_flag_small.png" class="ues-usa-img">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-8bd0555c

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,825 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 117,722 affected page loads) ~5KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~5,062 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 117,722 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-471eb018

Element path: div.col-12 > div.official-banner > div#accordion > a#banner-toggle

Snippet:

<a id="banner-toggle" data-toggle="collapse" href="#" data-target="#collapseOne" aria-expanded="true">

Label: Here's how you know.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.01:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0f68b8, surrounding text: #373a3c)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-627ccbfb

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,825 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 117,722 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-e42a2748

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-1022ac6d

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-16666ce6

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-be681717

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,537 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 115,324 affected page loads) ~6KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~6,689 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 115,324 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 DAP-6167e109

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator active" aria-label="Slide 1" data-slide="1">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-6167e109

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator" aria-label="Slide 2" data-slide="2">

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-e081d0a5

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Element 1 DAP-c581b385

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.tabs-container > input#consumers

Snippet:

<input class="radiotab" name="tabs" type="radio" id="consumers" checked="checked" aria-expanded="false" data-once="tab-click">

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-454ce981

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-07fca181

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list) DAP-9dceef9b

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-4ead3bcb

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

                                                sendEvent('Telephone Cli…" target="_self">

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-8aab4e3a

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-561944f4

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-267989d9

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-703deda8

`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title) DAP-3d25bc8d

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,087 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 108,656 affected page loads) ~326People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~326 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 108,656 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,390 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 108,656 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-895b4987

Element path: div.embedcore > div#embedcore-e1b008a88e > div#censusgov-iframe-G3AV8UJSU1XOUKXW9A > iframe

Snippet:

<iframe src="https://www.census.gov/populationwidget/populationwidget.php" height="100%" width="100%" allow="autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen="">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-2af64e05

Element path: div.embedcore > div#embedcore-b645a3e56a > div#censusgov-iframe-RY8L92G1QTTMRL7FRS > iframe

Snippet:

<iframe src="https://www.census.gov/econwidget?noheader" height="100%" width="100%" allow="autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen="">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-ef02d648

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-86e35c8e

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Element 1 DAP-9f9e7d68

Element path: div.uscb-footer-main > div.uscb-footer-follow > div.uscb-footer-social-icon-links > a.uscb-share-icon

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.facebook.com/uscensusbureau" target="_blank" onclick="linkClick( this, 'Social Links Footer' );" class="uscb-share-icon">

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Accessibility Findings

https://time.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 87

Axe findings: 3

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-e95a9017

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-c1ad9967

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.
